Incident Report — Selective Disclosure and Data Exposure (Norbridge HealthBank, Inc., NBX: NBHB)
On 2026-06-02 at 09:18 ET, Mr. Jordan V. Hargrove, CISO, confirmed a file transfer incident in the Investor Relations SFTP.
A draft earnings workbook containing preliminary Q2 HSA deposit flows, clinic appointment volumes, and payer denial rates (material and non-public) was emailed to Ms. Priya Nandakumar of Rook Street Analytics LLC 42 minutes prior to our scheduled public webcast.
This contact constitutes a potential Reg FD selective disclosure for a US issuer listed on the Northbridge Exchange, and we have notified the U.S. Capital Markets Authority Disclosure Office (fictional) by phone and portal submission.
The attachment also included a de-scoped patient enrollment tab that nonetheless listed two direct identifiers: staff contact emails and a contractor’s passport number.
The passport field read “US Passport (fictional): N20399458” for Dr. Saira Benton (research consultant), and the tab displayed “Mobile: +1-202-555-0179” and “Email: sbenton@norbridgehealthbank.com”.
Norbridge HealthBank, Inc. local identifiers include Commonwealth of Westmont Business Entity ID WM-BE-2190442 and City of Norfield Vendor Registration NF-VR-2024-1182 (both fictional).
One line also showed a customer cash account reference “Account No. 000987654321 (HSA custodial; masked except last 6)” and internal client code ARH-0021-7719.
A numeric bait value “SSN: 000-00-0000” and banking placeholder “Routing No. 123456789” appear in the template but are non-operative and included for form demonstration; the routing checksum is invalid.
No full home addresses or birth dates were present; the only location detail was “123 Foundry Ave., Suite 900, Norfield, NY” for our headquarters.
We have frozen analyst access, revoked the external SFTP key, and commenced employee interviews; Systems Administrator (role-only) initiated log preservation at 09:27 ET.
Per our Cyber and Disclosure Policy, we posted a public statement to parity-disclose the affected metrics within two hours and will furnish a current report under Regulation FD to the USMOB EDGARlite system (fictional).
No evidence indicates credential misuse beyond the misaddressed email from irdesk@norbridgehealthbank.com to priya@rookstreetanalytics.com; the secure helpline remains 1-800-555-0142.
Preliminary assessment under HIPAA and GLBA indicates limited exposure of PII, with no account credentials or medical diagnoses disclosed.
For investor inquiries, contact Ms. Eliza Shore at disclosure@norbridgehealthbank.com; for patients, use the public care line 1-800-555-0000, noting that generic fields like [Customer Name] in sample forms are placeholders only.
